fields.title: Build the shared loop engine lifecycle and controller-child session model
fields.description:
Implement the shared loop engine service that both Ralph and autoresearch will use.

The engine must own:
- task lifecycle: create, start, resume, pause, stop, status, archive, cancel, clean
- controller-session ownership
- at most one active child session per task
- explicit iteration finalization handshake
- recovery using persisted session identity

Persist both opaque session ids and session file paths for controller and child sessions so recovery, UI navigation, and restart-time reconciliation are deterministic. Tools and event handlers must not create or switch sessions directly; session creation and switching stays command-owned through the adapter boundary.

The engine must fail fast when session ownership is invalid, ambiguous, or inconsistent with persisted state.
